05_ALT_status_full

Andrea Polazzi

2026-04-16

Setup

Let’s load the dataset and have a look at it.

library(tidyverse)
## Warning: package 'ggplot2' was built under R version 4.5.2
## Warning: package 'tibble' was built under R version 4.5.2
## Warning: package 'tidyr' was built under R version 4.5.2
## Warning: package 'readr' was built under R version 4.5.2
## Warning: package 'purrr' was built under R version 4.5.2
## Warning: package 'dplyr' was built under R version 4.5.2
## Warning: package 'lubridate' was built under R version 4.5.2
## ── Attaching core tidyverse packages ──────────────────────── tidyverse 2.0.0 ──
## ✔ dplyr     1.2.0     ✔ readr     2.2.0
## ✔ forcats   1.0.1     ✔ stringr   1.6.0
## ✔ ggplot2   4.0.2     ✔ tibble    3.3.1
## ✔ lubridate 1.9.5     ✔ tidyr     1.3.2

## ✖ dplyr::filter() masks stats::filter()
## ✖ dplyr::lag()    masks stats::lag()
## ℹ Use the conflicted package (<http://conflicted.r-lib.org/>) to force all conflicts to become errors
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE)
library(readxl, quietly = TRUE)
data <- read_xlsx('../../data/raw/PCAWG_variables_PCA_woK.xlsx')
## Warning: Expecting numeric in I1255 / R1255C9: got 'NA'
## Warning: Expecting numeric in I1724 / R1724C9: got 'NA'
summary(data)

We have to rename columns, set categories as factors, deal with NA values, and handle typing.

##    donor_id                 cancer_type      alt_status      tf_rate

Define the full dataset

We must account for the fact that we have many NA values, especially in the TERT_FPKM column. These rows must be excluded, since further analyses such as PCA or RF require the absence of missing values. Therefore this extended model will use a subset of samples with complete telomere biology annotations.

##     alt_status     tf_rate         telomere_insertion_rate
##  ALT-low :768   Min.   : 0.00000   Min.   :0.00000        
##  ALT-high: 49   1st Qu.: 0.00000   1st Qu.:0.00000        
##                 Median : 0.03147   Median :0.00000        
##                 Mean   : 0.12777   Mean   :0.03592        
##                 3rd Qu.: 0.07243   3rd Qu.:0.01934        
##                 Max.   :19.73776   Max.   :3.46027        
##  telomere_content_log2   TERT_FPKM        ATAGGG_singleton_dist

##  Mean   :-0.44621      Mean   : 0.91717   Mean   : 0.01321

##  1st Qu.:-0.36756      1st Qu.:-0.40600     
##  Median :-0.01286      Median : 0.02340     
##  Mean   : 0.02180      Mean   : 0.00976     
##  3rd Qu.: 0.44543      3rd Qu.: 0.48155     
##  Max.   : 3.19366      Max.   : 2.25362

Now we don’t have NA values anymore, but we should be aware of the fact that we are dealing with a much smaller dataset (1943 before, 817 now) and some cancer types are underrepresented or totally absent.

Biplot

UMAP plot

Split the dataset

First classifier: Logistic Regression (all features)

Evaluate performance

## Area under the curve: 0.998

When the model is trained using the full set of telomere-related variables (TVR motifs, TERT expression, telomere fusion rate, telomere insertions, and telomere content), the ROC curve shows almost perfect separation between ALT-high and ALT-low tumors. The AUC of 0.998 indicates near-perfect discriminatory ability, meaning the model can almost always rank ALT-high tumors above ALT-low ones. Compared with the TVR-only model, this represents a dramatic improvement in predictive performance.

Other metrics

## Confusion Matrix and Statistics
## 
##           Reference
## Prediction ALT-low ALT-high
##   ALT-low      380        2
##   ALT-high       4       23
##                                           
##                Accuracy : 0.9853          
##                  95% CI : (0.9683, 0.9946)
##     No Information Rate : 0.9389          
##     P-Value [Acc > NIR] : 3.82e-06        
##                                           
##                   Kappa : 0.8768          
##                                           
##  Mcnemar's Test P-Value : 0.6831          
##                                           
##             Sensitivity : 0.92000         
##             Specificity : 0.98958         
##          Pos Pred Value : 0.85185         
##          Neg Pred Value : 0.99476         
##              Prevalence : 0.06112         
##          Detection Rate : 0.05623         
##    Detection Prevalence : 0.06601         
##       Balanced Accuracy : 0.95479         
##                                           
##        'Positive' Class : ALT-high        
##

The confusion matrix confirms the excellent performance of the logistic regression model when all telomere-related features are included. The model achieves an overall accuracy of 98.4% and a very high balanced accuracy of 99.1%, indicating strong performance for both classes despite the dataset imbalance. Notably, the model reaches perfect sensitivity (1.00) for this fold, correctly identifying all ALT-high tumors in the test set, while maintaining a high specificity of 98.3%, meaning that most ALT-low tumors are also classified correctly. The positive predictive value of 0.79 reflects the fact that some ALT-low tumors are still predicted as ALT-high, but the negative predictive value of 1.00 shows that tumors predicted as ALT-high are always correct in this sample.

Second classifier: Random Forest

## 
## Call:
##  randomForest(formula = alt_status ~ ., data = train, importance = T) 
##                Type of random forest: classification
##                      Number of trees: 500
## No. of variables tried at each split: 3
## 
##         OOB estimate of  error rate: 2.7%
## Confusion matrix:
##          ALT-low ALT-high class.error
## ALT-low      382        2 0.005208333
## ALT-high       9       15 0.375000000

Evaluate performance

## Area under the curve: 0.9843

Almost perfect (AUC = 0.9877). With this sample, our Random Forest model achieves almost perfect discriminatory ability.

Other metrics

## Confusion Matrix and Statistics
## 
##           Reference
## Prediction ALT-low ALT-high
##   ALT-low      383        9
##   ALT-high       1       16
##                                           
##                Accuracy : 0.9756          
##                  95% CI : (0.9555, 0.9882)
##     No Information Rate : 0.9389          
##     P-Value [Acc > NIR] : 0.0004346       
##                                           
##                   Kappa : 0.7495          
##                                           
##  Mcnemar's Test P-Value : 0.0268567       
##                                           
##             Sensitivity : 0.64000         
##             Specificity : 0.99740         
##          Pos Pred Value : 0.94118         
##          Neg Pred Value : 0.97704         
##              Prevalence : 0.06112         
##          Detection Rate : 0.03912         
##    Detection Prevalence : 0.04156         
##       Balanced Accuracy : 0.81870         
##                                           
##        'Positive' Class : ALT-high        
##

With this fold, the RF model achieved slightly worse performances with respect to Log. Reg., but still very good. We should be careful to not interpret this results as a general metric of performance, since we did not do CV yet.

Cross Validation of the performances

To assess the robustness of these two predictive models, we conducted repeated stratified cross-validation.

Logistic Regression - CV

## # A tibble: 25 × 10
##      auc sensitivity specificity precision recall    f1 balanced_accuracy   rep
##    <dbl>       <dbl>       <dbl>     <dbl>  <dbl> <dbl>             <dbl> <int>

## # ℹ 9 more variables: specificity_sd <dbl>, precision_mean <dbl>,
## #   precision_sd <dbl>, recall_mean <dbl>, recall_sd <dbl>, f1_mean <dbl>,
## #   f1_sd <dbl>, balanced_accuracy_mean <dbl>, balanced_accuracy_sd <dbl>
